mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in real GDP growth and industrial production indicates synchronized demand-supply expansion, which historically compresses equity risk premiums and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% and INDPRO exceeds 100
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations, or multivariate IC drops below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, further increases in the federal funds rate fail to anchor inflation expectations and instead trigger stagflation fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS rises alongside CPIAUCSL above the 90th percentile, or t-test p-value exceeds 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and consumer credit volumes reflect robust household spending power, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns in high-RSAFS months are not significantly higher than low-RSAFS months (Mann-Whitney p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production breaks above its long-term trend threshold of 100, it signals sustained manufacturing capacity utilization that reduces input cost inflation and supports equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in > 55% of instances where INDPRO crosses 100, or rolling 3-year IC falls below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong nonfarm payrolls growth and declining housing starts indicates an overheating labor market without corresponding real estate investment, which historically precedes equity drawdowns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.5% while HOUST declines
failure_condition: Joint divergence signal yields positive forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low interest rates, housing starts act as a leading indicator for construction and consumer durable demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as related sectors rally.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when HOUST rises in low-rate environments, or rolling 1-year IC remains below 0.03.
